homepage.title: Welcome to the London Secure Data Environment Data Portal
homepage.intro1: OneLondon has developed a world leading resource for health and care improvement known as the London Secure Data Environment (SDE). This is comprised of the London Data Service (LDS) and the London Analytics Platform (LAP).
homepage.intro2: The data collected from healthcare systems from across London by these services is used to improve patient care and support healthier communities across London.
homepage.intro3: This portal is where you can tell us if you don't want your data used by LDS or LAP for anything other than your direct healthcare. By clicking the start button below you will be taken through a process to exercise your right to request that we do not use your data for anything other than care provided to you by healthcare professionals. If you ever want to change that decision in the future you can go through the same process to tell us we can use your data.
homepage.intro4: By clicking the button below you can exercise your right to opt-out of your data being used for anything other than the direct care provided to you by healthcare professionals.
homepage.startButtonOther: Manage data sharing for another person
homepage.startButtonOtherDescription: Act on behalf of someone else to manage their NHS Data sharing preferences in the London Data Service, if you have the legal authority to represent them.
homepage.beforeYouStartList1: You will need the NHS number of the person you are representing
homepage.beforeYouStartList2: We will be sending an e-mail, SMS text message or letter to the contact details the person you are representing has registered with their GP. This will help us to confirm that we are in contact with the right person. You should be confident that this person has up-to-date contact details recorded with their GP.
homepage.beforeYouStartList3: The easiest way to identify the person you are representing is to provide their NHS number, if you do not have this you will need to provide other contact details.

expandableHeaders.section0: What is the London Secure Data Environment?
expandableHeaders.section1: What is the London Data Service?
expandableHeaders.section2: How will the London Data Service and the London Analytics Platform use my data?
expandableHeaders.section3: How do I stop the London Data Service and London Analytics Platform using my data?
expandableHeaders.section4: Where can I see the privacy notices for the London Data Service and London Analytics Platform?

homepage.yourChoice: Your choice will help determine how your information is used to support healthcare research and improvements across London.

section0.p1: The London Secure Data Environment (SDE) is a highly secure platform that allows approved organisations to access and analyse sensitive health and care data for London, without the data ever leaving the environment. It follows national NHS Secure Data Environment standards and is designed to protect patient privacy while enabling valuable insights for healthcare planning, research, and service improvement.
section0.p2: Rather than sharing copies of data, the SDE brings users to the data in a controlled, auditable setting. Access is tightly governed, data is pseudonymised where appropriate, and all outputs are checked before release, ensuring data is used safely, legally, and transparently.

section1.p1: The London Data Service securely collects patient information from a range of healthcare locations across London such as GP surgeries and hospitals. It then organises and stores this information ready to be used by other approved systems such as the London Care Record and London Analytics Platform to improve patient care and support healthier communities across London.

section2.p1: Both the London Data Service (LDS) and London Analytics Platform (LAP) only securely share identifiable data, data that can be used to tell someone who you are, with services that use your data to provide you with direct care.
section2.p2: Both the LDS and LAP only provide de-identified data to other approved services, which does not include information which could tell someone who you are (such as names, addresses and dates of birth). These approved services use the de-identified data for healthcare planning and design, population health management and healthcare research. This helps to better understand the needs of Londoners to more effectively plan and improve healthcare services. It also helps to understand more about different conditions and diseases, develop new treatments as well as run clinical trials.

section3.p1: You can use this portal to tell us that you do not want your data used for secondary purposes such as healthcare planning, population health management and research. To register your details to 'opt-out' click the button above.
section3.p2: Telling us that you don't want your data shared with healthcare professionals who will be treating you is a different process. To do that please e-mail NELondonicb.oneLondon.opt-out@nhs.net and make the subject of the e-mail "Dissent from data sharing for direct care".

section4.p1: The London Data Service and London Analytics Platform Privacy Notices are available
section4.p2: and the LDS Privacy notice is available

content.nhs: Your NHS number helps us locate your record quickly and accurately.
content.details: Make sure your name and date of birth are correct before proceeding.
content.confirm: Confirmation means you've reviewed and validated the information provided.
content.choice: Please indicate your preference regarding the opt-out.
content.notify: Please Confirm your selection and save.

SearchByNHSNumber.nhsNumberHint: You can find your NHS Number on letters from your GP or hospital, prescriptions, or in the NHS App.
SearchByNHSNumber.poaNhsNumberLabel: NHS Number of the Person You Are Representing

poaRelationshipOptions.parent: The patient is under 13 and you are their parent
poaRelationshipOptions.guardian: The patient is under 13 and you are their appointed guardian
poaRelationshipOptions.attorney: The patient is over 13 and you have power of attorney with the right to act on their behalf.

SearchByNHSNumber.idontknowButton: I Don't know their NHS Number
SearchByNHSNumber.errorRecaptchaLoad: Failed to load reCAPTCHA. Please refresh and try again.
SearchByNHSNumber.errorRecaptchaNotReady: reCAPTCHA is not ready. Please try again later.
SearchByNHSNumber.errorCreatePatient: Failed to create patient. Please try again.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidanceNhsNumberText1: Enter the patient's NHS Number. Tell them they can find it on a medical card, prescription, GP letter, hospital correspondence, or the NHS app.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidanceNhsNumberText2: Searching with an NHS Number helps us securely find the patient’s details. This helps ensure we match them to the correct record and keep their information safe.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidanceNhsNumberText3: If the patient does not know their NHS Number, you can still continue by clicking "I Don't know their NHS Number" below. This will direct you to search by name, date of birth, and postcode.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidanceNhsNumberText4: Their NHS Number will only be used to look up patient details and will not be shared outside the NHS.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidanceEligibilityHeading: You can only opt someone else out if:

helpGuidanceEligibilityList.parent: The patient is under 13 and you are their parent
helpGuidanceEligibilityList.guardian: The patient is under 13 and you are their appointed guardian
helpGuidanceEligibilityList.attorney: The patient is over 13 and you have power of attorney with the right to act on their behalf.

SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidanceEligibilityText: If you do not meet one of these criteria, you cannot opt someone else out.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidancePoaNhsNumberText1: Its easiest if the requestor provides the patient’s NHS Number. Tell them they can find it on medical cards, prescriptions, GP letters, hospital correspondence, or the NHS app.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidancePoaNhsNumberText2: If they do not know the NHS number click I Don't know their NHS Number to continue. You will need to tick the Requesting an Opt-out on someone else's behalf box on the next page.
SearchByNHSNumber.helpGuidancePoaNhsNumberText3: Put the details of the person making the request in the Firstname, Surname and Relationship fields.
SearchByNHSNumber.errorNoAccess: Current user doesn't have access to perform this function. Please contact helpdesk to request Agent access.

SearchByDetails.detailsOfPersonRepresenting: Details of the person you are representing
SearchByDetails.dobInvalid: Enter a valid date of birth
SearchByDetails.dobRealInvalid: Enter a real date of birth
SearchByDetails.submitError: Failed to create patient. Please try again.
SearchByDetails.poaRelationshipParent: The patient is under 13 and you are their parent
SearchByDetails.poaRelationshipGuardian: The patient is under 13 and you are their appointed guardian
SearchByDetails.poaRelationshipAttorney: The patient is over 13 and you have power of attorney with the right to act on their behalf.
SearchByDetails.helpGuidanceSubtitle: Requesting an Opt-out on someone else's behalf
SearchByDetails.helpGuidanceText1: You can make a request to opt-out on behalf of someone else to stop their personal data being used for secondary purposes if:
SearchByDetails.helpGuidanceText2: If you are in these circumstances then please enter your details in this blue box and in every other box use the patient's details.
SearchByDetails.helpGuidanceText3: If one of these circumstances does not describe you then you cannot opt someone else out. Please click the back button.

ConfirmDetails.isThisYou: Is this the person you are representing?
ConfirmDetails.name: Name
ConfirmDetails.useExistingCode: If you have already been sent a code to verify your identity
ConfirmDetails.reviewDetails: Please review the details shown on this page with the user. Ask THEM to provide it to you – don’t provide personal details to an unverified patient.
ConfirmDetails.detailsCorrect: If these details are correct and belong to the patient, click Yes to continue.
ConfirmDetails.detailsIncorrect: If these details do not match the patient, click No to return and try searching again with their NHS Number.
ConfirmDetails.alreadyReceivedCode: If these contact details are old and need updating tell the patient they will need to update the contact details that we hold on record with their GP. We do not have authority to edit their details.
ConfirmDetails.securityInfo: If the patient already has a code sent by SMS, email, or post, you can use it now by clicking the button below to go straight to the code confirmation page.
ConfirmDetails.nhsLoginParagraph1: The email address and phone number shown are taken from your NHS account settings.
ConfirmDetails.nhsLoginParagraph2: If these details are incorrect, you can update them
ConfirmDetails.nhsLoginParagraph3: Once updated, please sign out and sign back in to continue.
ConfirmDetails.nameOnlyMessage1: We can only confirm the patient's name and cannot use their contact details.
ConfirmDetails.nameOnlyMessage2: However you can confirm to the patient that their personal data is already opted-out of use for secondary purposes.

PositiveConfirmation.confirmationRequiredDescription: Please confirm these details are correct before continuing:
PositiveConfirmation.chooseMethod: We need to send a code to you, how would you like to receive it:
PositiveConfirmation.helpReceivingCodeDescription1: To keep your information secure, we need to send you a one-time code. This code helps us confirm your identity before you continue.
PositiveConfirmation.helpReceivingCodeDescription2: Please choose how you would like to receive your code: by Email, SMS, or Post (letter). Select the option that is most convenient and accessible for you.
PositiveConfirmation.helpReceivingCodeDescription3: Your details are only used for identification and will not be shared outside the NHS
PositiveConfirmation.helpReceivingCodeDescription4: If the Patient cannot access any of these methods they must provide you with a method of identity confirmation
PositiveConfirmation.noCodeRecieved: I haven't received my code, please can I request a new code?. Click
PositiveConfirmation.confirmNewCodeMessage: Are you sure you want to generate a new code? This will invalidate your previous code.
PositiveConfirmation.resendInfo: If you have already requested a code but haven't received it please click here to resend yourself a code.

ConfirmCode.errorEnterCode: Enter the 5 digit code sent to you
ConfirmCode.errorNoPatient: No patient found. Please restart the process.
ConfirmCode.errorGeneric: An error occurred. Please try again.
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeTitle: How does the patient get their code?
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeText1: A 5-digit code will have been sent to you by one of the following methods:
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeEmail: Email to your registered email address
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeLetter: Letter to your home address (please allow up to 3 days for delivery)
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeText2: Once you receive your code, please tell me the code to enter. This helps us confirm that we have the correct patient record and prove your identity.
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeText3: If the patient has not received their code, please tell them to:
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeText3Bullet1: E-mail: Check your spam or junk email folder
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeText3Bullet2: Letter: Allow extra time for a letter to arrive.
ConfirmCode.helpHowGetCodeText3Bullet3: SMS: Allow up to 20 minutes for the text to arrive
ConfirmCode.helpWrongCodeText: The code is case sensitive, use capitals. If you enter the wrong code 3 times the code will be locked and a new code must be generated.

OptOut.optOutDesc1: I do not want my non-identifiable personal data to used in the London Data Service for analysing London's population health conditions and use of the NHS, designing and purchasing healthcare services or for medical research purposes.
OptOut.optOutDesc2: I acknowledge that my personal data will continue to be shared to directly provide NHS healthcare services to me.
OptOut.optInDesc1: I want my non-identifiable personal data to be used in the London Data Service for analysing London's population health conditions and use of the NHS, designing and purchasing healthcare services or for medical research purposes.
OptOut.optInDesc2: I acknowledge that my personal data will continue to be shared to directly provide NHS healthcare services to me.
OptOut.errorSelectOption: Please select an option to continue.
OptOut.helpOptOutTitle: Explain to the patient what Opt-Out does.
OptOut.helpOptOutDesc1: Choosing to opt out of sharing your data will stop your anonymised health information being used for:
OptOut.helpOptOutList1: Supporting the provision of direct and proactive healthcare
OptOut.helpOptOutList2: Planning and Commissioning of Healthcare Services
OptOut.helpOptOutDesc2: Choosing to opt-out of sharing your data for secondary purposes means your personal health information will still be used to make sure you get the treatment and care you need. For example, your data may be shared if you need to be referred to hospital or get a prescription.

ConfirmAndSave.errorMissingNhsOrOption: NHS number and option are required.
ConfirmAndSave.errorSaveFailed: Sorry, we couldn't save your decision. Please try again.
ConfirmAndSave.howToBeNotifiedLabel: How would you like to be told when your instruction has been applied?
ConfirmAndSave.aboutThisStepDesc1: You are about to save the patient’s opt-out preference.
ConfirmAndSave.aboutThisStepDesc2: Please select how you would like to be notified when your data has flowed into The London Data Service. You can choose to receive updates by SMS, email, or letter.
ConfirmAndSave.helpSms: SMS - a text message will be sent to your telephone
ConfirmAndSave.helpEmail: Email - a message will be sent to your registered email address
ConfirmAndSave.helpLetter: Letter - a letter will be sent to your home address (please allow up to 3 days for delivery)
ConfirmAndSave.helpChangePrefs: Check with them that they are happy with that choice as it will affect how the patient’s data will be shared for secondary uses within The London Data Service.
ConfirmAndSave.helpChangePrefs2: They can log a new request at any time to change their notification preferences.
ConfirmAndSave.helpChangePrefs3: A confirmation message will be sent to let them know we have recorded their request. This will be sent by the same method we used to send their verification code.
ConfirmAndSave.needHelpDesc: If you have any questions or need assistance, please contact our helpdesk.

ThankyouScreen.title: Thank You!
ThankyouScreen.preferencesSaved: Your preference has been saved and your request is complete.
ThankyouScreen.appreciation: If you have chosen to receive notifications, you will be notified once your preference is applied.
ThankyouScreen.safeToClose: You may now safely close this window or browser tab.
ThankyouScreen.visitPrivacyDetails: for more information on how and when your choice will be applied.
ThankyouScreen.moreinfo: for more information on how and when your choice will be applied.

websitePrivacyNotice.title: Website Privacy Notice
websitePrivacyNotice.intro: This privacy notice explains how we collect, use and protect the personal information you provide when you use this website. It is written for patients and members of the public and follows clear, plain language so you can quickly understand what we do with your data.
websitePrivacyNotice.whoWeAre: The ONE London Opt In / Opt Out portal is provided on behalf of NHS organisations across London. The portal enables patients to exercise their right to choose how their confidential information is used for purposes beyond their individual care. The service is managed by the London Data Service (LDS) in partnership with NHS London Integrated Care Boards.
websitePrivacyNotice.personalDetails: Information such as your NHS number, name, date of birth, and postcode are collected to verify your identity and apply your preference correctly.
websitePrivacyNotice.howWeUseIntro: The information you provide is used solely to:
websitePrivacyNotice.howWeUse1: verify your identity to process your opt-in or opt-out request,
websitePrivacyNotice.howWeUse2: record and maintain your current data sharing preference,
websitePrivacyNotice.howWeUse3: support auditing and compliance with NHS information governance standards,
websitePrivacyNotice.howWeUse4: ensure the website is secure and functioning properly.
websitePrivacyNotice.updates: This notice may be updated from time to time to reflect changes in legislation or the way the service operates. The latest version will always be available on this page.
websitePrivacyNotice.nhsLoginIntro: If you access our service using NHS England services (such as NHS login), please note the following:

nhsLoginDetails.part1: Please note that if you access our service using your NHS login details, the identity verification services are managed by NHS England. NHS England is the controller for any personal information you provided to NHS England to get an NHS login account and verify your identity, and uses that personal information solely for that single purpose. For this personal information, our role is a “processor” only and we must act under the instructions provided by NHS England (as the “controller”) when verifying your identity. To see NHS login's Privacy Notice and Terms and Conditions, please
nhsLoginDetails.part2: . This restriction does not apply to the personal information you provide to us separately.

consentDenied.consentNotGiven: You have not agreed to share your data.
consentDenied.consentNotGivenP1: You have chosen not to allow NHS login to share your data to the London Data Service.  Unfortunately we cannot verify your identity and proceed to recording your data sharing decision without this information.
consentDenied.consentNotGivenP2: If you wish to use this service please login again and agree to share your NHS login information.

DevEnvironmentWarning.bannerMessage: THIS SITE IS FOR TESTING ONLY. DO NOT ENTER REAL PATIENT DATA.
